description: GET /contexts/search response whose 'next_cursor' is JSON null. The search-response schema types 'next_cursor' as a bare string ("type": "string" in acdp-search-response.schema.json), NOT as "type": ["string", "null"]. Absent optional fields MUST be omitted, never serialized as null (RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.2.1, absent-vs-null wire convention). A strict receiver MUST reject 'next_cursor: null' as schema_violation rather than coercing null to 'absent'.

expected.rationale: next_cursor is typed as a bare string. null is not a valid wire value for a non-nullable field; the only conformant way to express 'no next page' is to omit the key entirely. Coercing null to absent would force every receiver to special-case the coercion and would diverge from the canonical wire form. Contrast supersedes, whose schema type is string|null (RFC-ACDP-0002 §3.1) and which is therefore legitimately nullable.
